name: ReMe (MemoryScope)
tagline: Long-term and context memory for AI agents — remember across sessions, compact long chats
seoTitle: ReMe (MemoryScope): Memory Framework for AI Agents
metaDescription: ReMe (formerly MemoryScope) is an open-source Python memory toolkit for AI agents. File-based and vector memory, context compaction, and semantic recall.
overview: ReMe (formerly MemoryScope) is a Python memory framework for AI agents. It gives an agent long-term memory so that old conversations are compacted instead of lost, important facts are stored, and relevant context is recalled in later sessions. It targets two common problems: a limited context window and stateless sessions that always start from scratch.

It ships two memory systems. ReMeLight is file-based: memory is stored as readable Markdown and JSONL files (a MEMORY.md for long-term facts, a daily journal, and raw dialog logs) that you can open, edit, and copy. The vector-based ReMe system stores memories for semantic retrieval with hybrid vector plus BM25 search.

It fits the agent-memory category as a library you call from your own agent code rather than a hosted service. It suits developers who want personalized, persistent memory for assistants, coding helpers, or support bots, and it reports state-of-the-art results on the LoCoMo and HaluMem memory benchmarks.
features: File-based memory (ReMeLight): memory stored as editable Markdown and JSONL files you can read, edit, and copy to migrate
Vector-based memory (ReMe) with hybrid retrieval combining vector search and BM25
Context management: check_context, compact_memory, and compact_tool_result keep long conversations within the context window
pre_reasoning_hook bundles tool-result compaction, context checks, and memory summarization into one async call
Long-term memory persistence with semantic memory_search across past sessions
Reported state-of-the-art results on the LoCoMo and HaluMem benchmarks

gettingStarted.intro: ReMe is a Python 3.10+ library installed from PyPI or source. Install it, then wire one of its memory systems into your agent's reasoning loop.

steps.heading: Install from PyPI
steps.body: Install the package with the file-based memory extra, which is recommended for a quick start.

steps.heading: Or install from source
steps.body: Clone the repo and install in editable mode if you want to track the latest changes.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/agentscope-ai/ReMe.git
cd ReMe
pip install -e ".[light]"

steps.heading: Use file-based memory (ReMeLight)
steps.body: Create a ReMeLight instance and call pre_reasoning_hook to compact history and recall memory before the model reasons.
steps.code:
import asyncio
from reme.reme_light import ReMeLight

async def main():
    reme = ReMeLight(
        default_as_llm_config={"model_name": "qwen3.5-35b-a3b"},
        default_file_store_config={"fts_enabled": True, "vector_enabled": False},
        enable_load_env=True,
    )
    await reme.start()

    messages = [...]
    processed_messages, summary = await reme.pre_reasoning_hook(
        messages=messages,
        system_prompt="You are a helpful AI assistant.",
        compressed_summary="",
        max_input_length=128000,
    )
    await reme.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

steps.heading: Or use vector-based memory (ReMe)
steps.body: Configure an LLM and embedding model, then retrieve stored memories by query for a given user.
steps.code:
import asyncio
from reme import ReMe

async def main():
    reme = ReMe(
        working_dir=".reme",
        default_llm_config={"backend": "openai", "model_name": "qwen3.5-plus"},
        default_embedding_model_config={"backend": "openai", "model_name": "text-embedding-v4"},
    )
    await reme.start()

    memories = await reme.retrieve_memory(query="Python programming", user_name="alice")
    await reme.close()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

useCases: Give a personal assistant agent long-term memory of user preferences and past conversations across sessions
Keep a coding assistant aware of project context and code-style preferences from earlier sessions
Track a support bot's history of a user's issues and settings for more personalized replies
Compact long multi-turn dialogues so key information survives a limited context window
